title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Policy Recall
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a repository engineering contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.
Rank correctness, security, accessibility, performance, compatibility, and maintenance risks.
Define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Exception Policy trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.

content.text: For Exception Policy, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Exception Policy precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A disabled rule needs no explanation if CI passes.

stimulus.title: Exception Policy case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A disabled rule needs no explanation if CI passes.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence. Artifact under review: a repository engineering contract.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Exception Policy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.text: Every new repository should use the same maximum gate set. This fails the Repo Purpose contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Exception Policy applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.
